evidences.title: Josh Hart Minutes Streak Makes Knicks, NBA History
evidences.content: Hart has helped the Knicks at least somewhat keep pace in the crowded Eastern Conference playoff pictures, particularly in the interior, as he's averaging over 11 rebounds during this span. The Villanova alum, locked up for five years over the offseason, also picked up the third triple-double of his career with 19 rebounds, 13 points, and 10 assists in March 3's win in Cleveland, one earned with star teammate and podcast host Jalen Brunson sidelined by an opening-minute injury. [...] Hart's de facto promotion can be viewed as a blessing and a curse: the Knicks, floundering without starters like OG Anunoby and Julius Randle, would no doubt have sunken further without Hart, but it would appear that the 2023 deadline acquisition is the latest to "earn" overtime for head coach Tom Thibodeau, who has been accused of relying too much on his starters. The Knicks are planning on making a playoff push and they will need Hart if they want a chance to make a deep run. Thibodeau, of course, is no stranger to high-profile injuries: Randle, for example, dislocated his shoulder in the final stages of a one-sided win over Miami on Jan. 27 and Derrick Rose's 2010-11 MVP campaign ended in heartbr...

evidences.title: Josh Hart is the Knicks’ imperfect answer to a Finals question - Yahoo Sports
evidences.content: Hart’s shooting hasn’t carried over from the regular season to the postseason. He averaged 12.0 points, 7.4 rebounds and 4.8 assists in 30.2 minutes during the regular season while shooting 50.8% from the field, 41.3% from 3-point range and 61.2% true shooting. Through 14 playoff games, he’s averaging 11.4 points, 8.6 rebounds and 4.6 assists in 33.0 minutes while shooting 44.3% from the field, 30.3% from deep and 52.8% true shooting. The Knicks have played him more while getting less efficient offense from him. That only happens when the rest of his impact has been too valuable to ignore. And that’s what the Knicks will need from him in the NBA Finals. [...] NBA image NBA image ... Fantasy image Fantasy image Watch image Watch image Network image Network image NY Daily News NY Daily News # Josh Hart is the Knicks’ imperfect answer to a Finals question The dare always finds Josh Hart eventually. It arrives when a defender rotates toward Jalen Brunson. It appears when the paint gets crowded, when help comes from the corner, when a scouting report decides the Knicks can live or die with Hart’s jumper. The Cleveland Cavaliers tested that bet in the Eastern Conference finals. Hart g...

evidences.title: Josh Hart - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Hart was selected in the first round of the 2017 NBA draft by the Utah Jazz with the 30th overall pick. He was subsequently traded to the Los Angeles Lakers along with the 42nd overall pick, Thomas Bryant "Thomas Bryant (basketball)"), in exchange for the Lakers' 28th overall pick, Tony Bradley "Tony Bradley (basketball)"). In his first start on December 14, 2017, Hart recorded a double-double against the Cleveland Cavaliers, scoring 11 points and grabbing 10 rebounds. Hart fractured his left hand in practice on February 28 and was sidelined until the end of March. In his final game as a rookie, Hart scored a career-high 30 points against the Los Angeles Clippers, shooting 7 of 9 from three. Hart finished the season averaging 7.9 points per game and 4.2 rebounds per game, recording 8 [...] Joshua Aaron Hart (born March 6, 1995) is an American professional basketball player for the New York Knicks of the National Basketball Association (NBA). Across both his college and professional careers, he has logged minutes at shooting guard and both forward positions and is known for his hustle plays and rebounding ability. He holds the Knicks franchise record for the most triple-doubles i...

evidences.title: Knicks NBA Finals Schedule: Everything We Know So Far (Updated)
evidences.content: In this story: New York Knicks After sweeping the Cleveland Cavaliers in the Eastern Conference Finals, the Knicks will get over a week off to rest. And while eight days of recovery will be great for OG Anunoby's hamstring, it will leave plenty of fans counting down the days to the next time the Knicks take the court. While the Thunder and Spurs are still battling it out in the Western Conference Finals (and Knicks fans will be hoping that series goes long and takes a toll on the eventual winner), most of the details for the Knicks' upcoming NBA Finals schedule are already official. We know the date and start time for every game, as well as the general breakdown of home and road games. ## Dates, start times and TV channel [...] ## Dates, start times and TV channel Game 1: Wednesday June 3, 8:30 p.m. ET (ABC) Game 2: Friday June 5, 8:30 p.m. ET (ABC) Game 3: Monday June 8, 8:30 p.m. ET (ABC) Game 4: Wednesday June 10, 8:30 p.m. ET (ABC) Game 5: Saturday June 13, 8:30 p.m. ET (ABC) - if needed Game 6: Tuesday June 16, 8:30 p.m. ET (ABC) - if needed Game 7: Friday June 19, 8:30 p.m. ET (ABC) - if needed The NBA Finals broadcast schedule is more consistent than previous rounds', wit...

evidences.title: ClutchPoints - This could actually change everything for...
evidences.content: ## ClutchPoints's Post ### ClutchPoints March 28 · This could actually change everything for the Knicks. Josh Hart was never known as a shooter. Teams even cheat on defense by leaving him wide open and dare him to shoot threes. That strategy hasn't worked much this season. Hart knew he had to work on his three-point shooting in the offseason and that's exactly what he did. Mike Brown gave him the green light and Hart responded by having the best shooting stretch of his career. ❄️ Career 3P% before this season with at least 200 3PA: 33.2% 🔥 3P% this season with 217 attempts after 59 games: 40.6% Goes to show that anything can be improved as long as long as you actually work on it. Ben Simmons needs to take notes. 📝 No photo description available. [...] All reactions: 714 18 comments 32 shares Like Comment Most relevant ﻿ Top fan Chuck Craddock The word you never need. evidences.title: NBA announces Finals schedule after Knicks knock off Cavs | theScore
evidences.content: Game 1 of the NBA Finals tips off June 3 at 8:30 p.m. ET. The Knicks get nine days' rest before their next game after reeling off 11-straight playoff victories. The first NBA Finals contest played at Madison Square Garden since the Spurs and David Robinson knocked off New York in Game 5 back in 1999 will be June 8. San Antonio and the Knicks split their regular-season games 1-1, while the Thunder beat New York in both games.
